title: malus sylvestris subsp. mitis
intro: Malus sylvestris subsp. mitis, also known as the wild apple or crabapple, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Rosaceae family. The medicinal properties of Malus sylvestris subsp. mitis include its high content of polyphenols, flavonoids, and anthocyanins, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Malus sylvestris subsp. mitis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in jams, preserves, and other food products to leverage its medicinal and nutritional value.
